jokes.text: You're talking about me in morse code.
jokes.setup: Jim paranoidly accuses coworkers of using morse code
jokes.punchline: Reveals his deep insecurity about being discussed
jokes.explanation: Good paranoid character moment with absurd accusation, well-delivered

jokes.text: Well, you know what? Joke's on you. 'cause I know morse code. Ha!
jokes.setup: Jim's defensive response to imaginary morse code
jokes.punchline: Proudly declares he knows morse code as if it's a gotcha
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity but feels more forced than the initial accusation

jokes.text: In our very limited free time And with our very limited budget, We went and got a nanny and then we went out And took a class on a very outmoded And very unnecessary form of communication just so We could talk about you in front of you.
jokes.setup: Jim's absurd morse code accusation
jokes.punchline: Pam sarcastically lists the ridiculous steps they'd have to take
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character voice - Pam's dry sarcasm perfectly deflates Jim's paranoia

jokes.text: It all started when dwight was tapping his foot Against the leg of his desk. When I asked him to stop, he said, 'I will when you lose the baby weight.'
jokes.setup: Jim explaining the origin of his morse code paranoia
jokes.punchline: Reveals Dwight's cruel baby weight comment as the real trigger
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing how Dwight's cruelty triggered Jim's paranoia

jokes.text: Very well. I must have imagined it. I apologize.
jokes.setup: Pam's devastating sarcastic response
jokes.punchline: Jim's deflated, formal apology
jokes.explanation: Nice button on the sequence - Jim's formal language shows his embarrassment

jokes.text: Detonator. / Detonator where? / Michael! / Jim. Are you clicking a detonator? / - It's a pen. - Michael, come on.
jokes.setup: Dwight sees Jim clicking a pen
jokes.punchline: Dwight thinks it's a detonator, escalating to involve Michael
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight paranoia escalating mundane office behavior into security threat

jokes.text: Hey. Tap away.
jokes.setup: Previous morse code paranoia sequence
jokes.punchline: Jim gives permission for Dwight to tap, showing he's moved past it
jokes.explanation: Nice callback showing character growth - Jim's moved past his paranoia

jokes.text: Ooh! Things with donna are so-- Oh-ho-ho! They're going great. I, uh... We're just clicking on every level. Emotionally and sexually and...Orally
jokes.setup: Michael sharing about his relationship
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause before adding 'orally' as third category
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael oversharing - the pause makes it funnier but it's predictable territory

jokes.text: And I am not used to relationships
jokes.setup: Michael talking about his success with Donna
jokes.punchline: Cuts to talking head mid-sentence, implying he was cut off
jokes.explanation: Good editing joke - the abrupt cut suggests he said something inappropriate

jokes.text: She has a pilates butt.
jokes.setup: Michael describing Donna's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and inappropriate description
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate specificity - economical and character-driven

jokes.text: But we need to find something to do this weekend Beside have sex. Did I say that? Yes, I did.
jokes.setup: Michael asking for weekend activity suggestions
jokes.punchline: Acknowledges he just overshared about sex to his employees
jokes.explanation: Self-aware Michael moment but not particularly original or impactful

jokes.text: Let me go back to my desk right now. / Okay, you get out of here, big dog.
jokes.setup: Stanley offers to leave the awkward meeting
jokes.punchline: Michael dismisses him with 'big dog' - Stanley doesn't want to help anyway
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Stanley trying to escape and Michael's oblivious nickname

jokes.text: stanley doesn't help with anything.
jokes.setup: Stanley leaving the meeting
jokes.punchline: Michael's matter-of-fact assessment of Stanley's work ethic
jokes.explanation: Perfect economical character observation - true to both Michael and Stanley

jokes.text: Room enough for three.
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting heart-shaped jacuzzis in the Poconos
jokes.punchline: Packer adds inappropriate suggestion about threesome
jokes.explanation: Very character-appropriate for Packer but predictable crude humor

jokes.text: Because when your super hot girlfriend says, 'I wanna go to mount pocono,' You go to mount pocono and you do her.
jokes.setup: Question about why go to Poconos for P.F. Chang's
jokes.punchline: Michael's crude explanation with awkward emphasis on 'do her'
jokes.explanation: Standard Michael crude overshare - character-appropriate but not clever

jokes.text: And we screwed. Whoops. Tmi.
jokes.setup: Michael already oversharing about sex
jokes.punchline: Adds more explicit detail then fake-apologizes for TMI
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation - the 'whoops TMI' feels forced and obvious

jokes.text: You know what, kelly? This is the real world. Not the real world: Scranton.
jokes.setup: Kelly suggesting Donna might be cheating
jokes.punchline: Michael references the MTV show while dismissing her concern
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting reality TV show to their situation in Scranton

jokes.text: When ryan had two girlfriends, He used to take me to some diner in hazelton Just so the other girl wouldn't see. / Some diner? It was the starlight diner. It's, it's in a life magazine spread About americana.
jokes.setup: Kelly reveals Ryan took her to distant diner to hide her
jokes.punchline: Ryan defensively elevates the 'some diner' to prestigious establishment
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - Ryan's vanity makes him defend the venue while missing the point

jokes.text: Nine dates. I said it on the second date. / That seems...Quick. Even for lesbians.
jokes.setup: Michael said 'I love you' on second date
jokes.punchline: Oscar's measured response with unexpected lesbian comparison
jokes.explanation: Great Oscar line - his measured delivery and unexpected reference point lands perfectly

jokes.text: yeah, she does all that.
jokes.setup: Rapid-fire list of cheating signs from office
jokes.punchline: Michael confirms Donna exhibits ALL the suspicious behaviors
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the list - Michael's matter-of-fact confirmation of obvious red flags

jokes.text: Run what? / Amok. It means, don't let your imagination run out of control. / Why didn't you just say that, pam?
jokes.setup: Pam tells Michael not to let imagination 'run amok'
jokes.punchline: Michael doesn't know 'amok' then complains about Pam not using simpler words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - his limited vocabulary and blame-shifting to Pam

jokes.text: Well, that's easy for you to say. You have a bad imagination. It's stupid. I live in a fantasy world.
jokes.setup: Pam telling Michael to control his imagination
jokes.punchline: Michael insults Pam's imagination then proudly claims he lives in fantasy
jokes.explanation: Excellent Michael logic - insults Pam then accidentally reveals his own delusion

jokes.text: But you didn't believe any of this was true Five minutes ago. / That's what makes it so wrong.
jokes.setup: Jim points out Michael's sudden 180 on the cheating theory
jokes.punchline: Michael's illogical reasoning that his quick change makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Michael logic - his emotional reasoning completely defies rational thought

jokes.text: Oh, god, that tickles. What did--
jokes.setup: Michael appears to be getting some kind of physical treatment
jokes.punchline: His reaction suggests inappropriate touching or medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Generic physical comedy moment without much substance or character specificity

jokes.text: $100 a day, plus expenses. / I'll give you $50. Money's no object.
jokes.setup: Dwight quotes his PI rate
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately cuts rate in half then says money's no object
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael contradiction - cheap behavior immediately followed by generous claim

jokes.text: You might not like what I find. / And you might not like how I find it.
jokes.setup: Standard PI warning about not liking results
jokes.punchline: Dwight adds ominous warning about his methods
jokes.explanation: Great Dwight moment - adding sinister warning about his investigative methods

jokes.text: My doctor told me to cut out hot dogs. We all got problems.
jokes.setup: Jim telling Stanley about printer catching fire
jokes.punchline: Stanley equates serious safety issue with his dietary restrictions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley line - his deadpan dismissal and personal priority shows his character beautifully

jokes.text: I would even settle for apricot preserves.
jokes.setup: Gabe asking about almond butter during serious printer discussion
jokes.punchline: His willingness to settle for preserves while ignoring safety issue
jokes.explanation: Good Gabe character moment - his obliviousness to priorities while focused on snacks

jokes.text: Two years ago, Andy blamed the warehouse for a late shipment That he forgot to process. We got yelled at pretty bad. Almost lost my job, And I was mad as hell at the time. But I said, 'darryl, just wait. he's a fool, there's gonna be an opportunity. Just be patient.'
jokes.setup: Darryl describing Andy screwing him over years ago
jokes.punchline: His patient revenge plan finally coming to fruition
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup for Darryl's revenge plot - patient character development pays off

jokes.text: All cases are solved with logic. The only logical way to find out If donna is a cheater is to seduce her, Bring her to orgasm, then call michael And tell him the sad news.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his investigative approach
jokes.punchline: His 'logical' plan involves seducing and sleeping with Donna
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - completely inappropriate plan delivered as rational investigation

jokes.text: It's never too early for ice cream, jim. But we didn't have any ice cream, So this is mayonnaise and black olives.
jokes.setup: Jim questioning Michael eating 'ice cream' early
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's eating mayonnaise and olives as comfort food
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disgusting Michael moment - his desperate improvisation for comfort food is brilliant

jokes.text: It's comfort food, all right? God.
jokes.setup: Disgusted reactions to mayo and olives
jokes.punchline: Michael defensively justifies his revolting 'comfort food'
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to the mayo reveal - Michael's defensive rationalization

jokes.text: Otherwise this thing is gonna spiral out of amok.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to use 'amok' correctly
jokes.punchline: Uses it incorrectly as 'out of amok'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Michael trying to use the word he just learned but still getting it wrong

jokes.text: Helene? / My mother. / Oh. My mother, helene. / Oh, yeah. Yes, yes. Yes. All of, all of the greatest loves of my life.
jokes.setup: Michael listing great loves including 'Helene'
jokes.punchline: Reveals Helene is his mother and includes her in romantic loves list
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael inappropriate boundary - including his mother in list of romantic loves

jokes.text: Hey, you know an exercise for two people That uses the whole body? / Yeah, I think I know what you're talking about. / Tractor pulling.
jokes.setup: Dwight's leading question about two-person exercise
jokes.punchline: Donna thinks sex, Dwight means literal tractor pulling
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - perfectly Dwight to suggest actual farm work while attempting seduction

jokes.text: One thing you need to know about me, I don't quit until something tears or pops.
jokes.setup: Dwight at gym trying to impress Donna
jokes.punchline: His disturbing description of workout intensity
jokes.explanation: Great Dwight line - his intense physicality described in disturbing terms

jokes.text: Tomorrow's fertilizer, am I right?
jokes.setup: Dwight getting kicked out of gym
jokes.punchline: His bizarre attempt at gym small talk about bodily functions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - his attempt at casual gym talk comes out as farm-related body function reference

jokes.text: We both know why I'm here: To see...You...Naked... While...I'm...Naked.
jokes.setup: Dwight's final desperate attempt to seduce Donna
jokes.punchline: His incredibly awkward, stilted delivery of sexual proposition
jokes.explanation: Peak awkward Dwight - his complete inability to be smooth in romantic situations

jokes.text: Who eats eight protein bars? / People who don't trust egg whites.
jokes.setup: Michael questioning Dwight's expense report
jokes.punchline: Dwight's paranoid explanation for protein bar consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight paranoia - his food trust issues explained in characteristic fashion

jokes.text: I am not paying for that membership.
jokes.setup: Dwight revealing he joined gym and will bill Michael monthly
jokes.punchline: Michael's indignant rejection in talking head
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat - Michael's penny-pinching showing through

jokes.text: Maybe you'd be more comfortable in your own office. / No, I like the attention.
jokes.setup: Michael groaning and being dramatic about his pulled muscles
jokes.punchline: Admits he's performing for attention rather than actually in pain
jokes.explanation: Good Michael moment - his honesty about attention-seeking behavior

jokes.text: You work at an adult arcade.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why Donna could have any man
jokes.punchline: His euphemistic description of what's presumably a casino or similar
jokes.explanation: Funny Michael euphemism - his innocent way of describing Donna's workplace

jokes.text: Well, you are boner-ific.
jokes.setup: Michael's self-deprecating plea for Donna not to dump him
jokes.punchline: Donna's crude but affectionate compliment using made-up word
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate for Donna - the made-up word lands decently

jokes.text: Poop my pants.
jokes.setup: Donna asking what Michael would do about going away together
jokes.punchline: Michael's crude expression of excitement
jokes.explanation: Standard crude Michael response - economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: How about, andy is a problem and we must eliminate him?
jokes.setup: Andy trying to interpret mumbled conversation about him
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests the most sinister possible interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from Jim - taking Andy's paranoia to darkly comic extreme

jokes.text: Whoa. I hadn't even thought of that.
jokes.setup: Jim suggesting Andy should be eliminated
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent admission he didn't consider the obvious interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good Andy character moment - his oblivious response to dark suggestion

jokes.text: Claire's? Zales? Ricky's? Earrings earrings earrings? Fancy girl? Platinum cat? Where?
jokes.setup: Pam asking where Donna got earrings
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire list of increasingly absurd store names
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with 'Earrings earrings earrings' and 'Platinum cat' being highlights

jokes.text: Okay, heart-shaped jewelry Is not something that a woman buys for herself. A man definitely bought it for her recently, And it wasn't michael.
jokes.setup: Pam grilling Donna about her earrings
jokes.punchline: Pam's detective work revealing the cheating evidence
jokes.explanation: Good detective work from Pam - logical deduction about the jewelry

jokes.text: Darnell's a chump. I would have done it for anything. I've done a lot more for a lot less.
jokes.setup: Creed explaining Darryl paid him to mess with Andy
jokes.punchline: Implies he's done worse things for no money at all
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed line - his criminal past implied through casual admission

jokes.text: It's cece. She's never gonna do anything wrong.
jokes.setup: Jim showing photos from Pam's Facebook investigation
jokes.punchline: Jim's proud father moment about his baby's perfection
jokes.explanation: Sweet Jim dad moment - his innocent pride in baby during serious investigation

jokes.text: Yes, I desperately want to speak with you About my sex life with jim.
jokes.setup: Michael assuming Pam wants relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Pam's sarcastic agreement to discuss her sex life
jokes.explanation: Good Pam sarcasm - her way of deflecting Michael's inappropriate assumptions

jokes.text: I swear too much.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why he gave away his golf clubs
jokes.punchline: His self-aware admission about his swearing problem
jokes.explanation: Great economical Michael line - unexpected self-awareness about his behavior

jokes.text: Just a notch above toby.
jokes.setup: Michael seeing Donna with another man
jokes.punchline: Uses his hatred of Toby as measurement for how low someone is
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to running gag - using Toby hatred as moral ranking system

jokes.text: That's good for five bucks at dunkin' donuts. Any dunkin' donuts.
jokes.setup: Gabe giving Andy a reward for bringing up printer issue
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small reward presented as generous gift
jokes.explanation: Good Gabe moment - his corporate cheapness disguised as generosity

jokes.text: Spice girls are opening for weird al. Front row.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting concert for Friday night
jokes.punchline: Bizarre lineup with Spice Girls opening for Weird Al
jokes.explanation: Great absurd Michael detail - the impossible concert lineup fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: You're cheating on me. / How do you know? / Pam told me.
jokes.setup: Michael's dramatic accusation of cheating
jokes.punchline: Donna doesn't deny it, just asks how he knows; credits Pam
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Donna's response confirms guilt while deflecting to method

jokes.text: I don't wanna prank anymore. Things get real. It's not funny. I'm just gonna be good, stay in my room, Go to church, Try to do one nice thing per day. I do not wanna prank anymore.
jokes.setup: Printer actually catching fire during prank
jokes.punchline: Darryl's moral crisis and vow to reform his life
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Darryl's genuine fear when prank becomes real danger

jokes.text: I'm the mistress?
jokes.setup: Michael learning Donna is married
jokes.punchline: His realization that he's the other man, not her victim
jokes.explanation: Perfect plot twist and character moment - Michael's shocked realization he's the homewrecker is brilliantly executed
